lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Barfy Bill
lines.text: Oh! Hello there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Who are you?

lines.speaker: Barfy Bill
lines.text: My name is Ex Sea Captain Barfy Bill.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ex sea captain?

lines.speaker: Barfy Bill
lines.text: Yeah, I bought a lovely ship and was planning to make a fortune running her as a merchant vessel.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why are you not still sailing?

lines.speaker: Barfy Bill
lines.text: Chronic sea sickness. My first, and only, voyage was spent dry heaving over the rails.

lines.speaker: Barfy Bill
lines.text: If I had known about the sea sickness I could have saved myself a lot of money.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What are you up to now then?

lines.speaker: Barfy Bill
lines.text: Well my ship had a little fire related problem. Fortunately it was well insured.

lines.speaker: Barfy Bill
lines.text: Anyway, I don't have to work anymore so I've taken to canoeing on the river.

lines.speaker: Barfy Bill
lines.text: I don't get river sick!

lines.speaker: Barfy Bill
lines.text: Would you like to know how to make a canoe?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Could you teach me about canoes?

lines.speaker: Barfy Bill
lines.text: Well, you don't look like you have the skill to make a canoe.

lines.speaker: Barfy Bill
lines.text: You need to have at least level 12 woodcutting.

lines.speaker: Barfy Bill
lines.text: Once you are able to make a canoe it makes travel along the river much quicker!

lines.speaker: Barfy Bill
lines.text: It's really quite simple. Just walk down to that tree on the bank and chop it down.

lines.speaker: Barfy Bill
lines.text: When you have done that you can shape the log further with your axe to make a canoe.

lines.speaker: Barfy Bill
lines.text: Hah! I can tell just by looking that you lack talent in woodcutting.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you mean?

lines.speaker: Barfy Bill
lines.text: No Callouses! No Splinters! No camp fires littering the trail behind you.

lines.speaker: Barfy Bill
lines.text: Anyway, the only 'canoe' you can make is a log. You'll be able to travel 1 stop along the river with a log canoe.

lines.speaker: Barfy Bill
lines.text: With your skill in woodcutting you could make my favourite canoe, the Dugout. They might not be the best canoe on the river, but they get you where you're going.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How far will I be able to go in a Dugout canoe?

lines.speaker: Barfy Bill
lines.text: You will be able to travel 2 stops on the river.

lines.speaker: Barfy Bill
lines.text: The best canoe you can make is a Stable Dugout, one step beyond a normal Dugout.

lines.speaker: Barfy Bill
lines.text: With a Stable Dugout you can travel to any place on the river.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Even into the Wilderness?

lines.speaker: Barfy Bill
lines.text: Not likely! I've heard tell of a man up near Edgeville who claims he can use a Waka to get up into the Wilderness.

lines.speaker: Barfy Bill
lines.text: I can't think why anyone would wish to venture into that hellish landscape though.

lines.speaker: Barfy Bill
lines.text: Hoo! You look like you know which end of an axe is which!

lines.speaker: Barfy Bill
lines.text: You can easily build one of those Wakas. Be careful if you travel into the Wilderness though.

lines.speaker: Barfy Bill
lines.text: I've heard tell of great evil in that blasted wasteland.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks for the warning Bill.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No thanks, not right now.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you teach me about canoes?
